message:
fix(cli): propagate SQL and dot-command errors to shell exit code

The interactive shell previously swallowed errors from execute_sql(),
.read, .open, .schema, and .dump -- always returning exit code 0 in
batch mode regardless of whether any statement failed. This made it
impossible for scripts piping SQL into fsqlite to detect failures.

A ShellOutcome struct replaces the bare ShellFlow return, carrying a
had_error flag that accumulates across the session. In batch mode
(fail_on_error=true, set automatically for non-interactive stdin and
-c command mode), any accumulated error causes exit code 1. Interactive
mode preserves the previous lenient behavior.

Error tracking is threaded through try_execute_dot_command via a
&mut bool parameter so that .read file-not-found, .open failures,
.schema errors, and .dump errors all contribute to the exit status.

New tests verify:
- -c with a failing SQL statement returns exit code 1
- .read of a missing file in batch mode returns exit code 1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
